body:
### Problem

after open the zsh completion file `_niri`(the file is too huge to put in the issue`)
then neovim panic
error report

```
nvim: /home/sanae/.cache/nvim/tree-sitter-zsh/src/scanner.c:475: deserialize: Assertion `size == length' failed.
```
### Steps to reproduce

nvim --embed _niri(the file i mentiond before)
then it panic , simplely

### Expected behavior

not panic

### Nvim version (nvim -v)

0.12.1

### Vim (not Nvim) behaves the same?

no, 9.2

### Operating system/version

Arch Linux zen kernel 6.19.11

### Terminal name/version

kitty

### $TERM environment variable

xterm-kitty

### Installation

arch linux pacman extra repository

comment_data.body:
#5  0x00007f5b77c0415d tree_sitter_zsh_external_scanner_deserialize.cold (zsh.so + 0x415d)

Looks like a third-party parser bug?
Yep, that is an issue with the tree-sitter-zsh parser (possibly https://github.com/georgeharker/tree-sitter-zsh/issues/33). Nothing we can do about this.

labels.description: Needs a third-party / external change or fix
labels.name: status:blocked-external

labels.description: We need minimal steps to reproduce the issue
labels.name: needs:repro

milestone.description: We don't plan to work on this, but will accept high quality contributions from someone who will own the feature and follow up on bug reports.
milestone.title: needs-owner

title: crash in tree-sitter-zsh parser (after opening very big zsh completion file)
